name.fr: Chipa (pain au fromage paraguayen au manioc)

description.en: Paraguay's gluten-free cheese bread: cassava starch, egg and plenty of cheese worked into a dough and shaped into small rings. Firmer and less stretchy than Brazil's pao de queijo, often lifted with a note of anise.
description.de: Paraguays glutenfreies Käsebrot: Maniokstärke, Ei und reichlich Käse werden zu einem Teig verarbeitet und zu kleinen Ringen geformt. Fester und weniger dehnbar als Brasiliens Pão de Queijo, oft mit einer Note Anis verfeinert.
description.es: El pan de queso sin gluten de Paraguay: almidón de mandioca, huevo y abundante queso se trabajan en una masa y se moldean en pequeños anillos. Más firme y menos elástico que el pao de queijo brasileño, a menudo con un toque de anís.
description.fr: Le pain au fromage sans gluten du Paraguay : fécule de manioc, œuf et beaucoup de fromage sont travaillés en une pâte façonnée en petits anneaux. Plus ferme et moins élastique que le pão de queijo brésilien, souvent relevé d'une note d'anis.

origin.note: Distinct from Brazil's pao de queijo: firmer, less stretchy, shaped into rings rather than small balls, and often flavored with anise. A year-round snack, especially popular around Easter.

yield.shape: small rings (rosquitas), rolled and joined into a loop

keeping.note: Best eaten warm the day it's baked while the cheesy chew is at its peak; reheats acceptably from frozen.

method.title: Mix wet
method.summary: Whisk the eggs, oil and milk together.

method.title: Add starch
method.summary: Whisk in half the cassava starch with the baking powder until smooth.

method.title: Finish dough
method.summary: Work in the remaining starch, the grated cheese, salt and anise seeds until a pliable dough forms.

method.title: Shape rings
method.summary: Divide into small portions, roll each into a short strip, and join the ends into a ring.

method.title: Space & bake
method.summary: Arrange the rings on a lined tray with space between them and bake until golden, 15-20 minutes.

method.title: Serve warm
method.summary: Eat warm as a snack, alongside coffee or cocido.

source.adaptation: Source's vague 'pinch' of salt standardized to a small defined baker's percentage, reflecting that the cheese itself supplies much of the seasoning.
